title: carex ferruginea subsp. kerneri
intro: Carex ferruginea subsp. kerneri, also known as Kern's sedge or rusty sedge, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the sedge family, native to Europe. The plant has been used for centuries in traditional medicine, valued for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which make it useful for treating wounds, skin irritations, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Carex ferruginea subsp. kerneri include its ability to soothe coughs, reduce fever, and act as an expectorant to help clear mucus from the lungs. In herbal preparations, the plant's roots and rhizomes are typically dried and used to make infusions, decoctions, or tinctures, which can be used to treat a range of ailments.
